LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VERA CORT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO APRIL 23, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT VERA CORT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE LEGISLATURE CHOSE TO LIMIT ITS EXCEPTION FOR TAX MATTERS TO THOSE ARISING UNDER THE REVENUE AND TAXATION CODE. (GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 12651(F).) THE COURT CANNOT ASSUME THE LEGISLATURE MADE A MISTAKE AND BROADEN ITS PLAIN AND UNAMBIGUOUS EXCEPTION TO INCLUDE THE SAN FRANCISCO BUSINESS AND TAX REGULATIONS CODE. ADDITIONALLY, IN SURREPLY, DEFENDANT CONTENDS THAT TAX LIABILITY IS NOT AN OBLIGATION THAT ARISES FROM STATUTE OR REGULATION. (GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 12650(B)(5).) EVEN IF THE PROCEDURAL DEFECTS OF SURREPLIES ARE IGNORED, THIS ARGUMENT FAILS ON ITS MERITS, AT LEAST AT THE DEMURRER STAGE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PETER J BUSCH, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Apr-17-2014 Continued To Aug-18-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defts And Her Attorneys
Off Calendar Apr-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS J. LALANNE, TEL. NO. 434-1122 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARK R. MEYER OF THE LOSCH & EHRLICH LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 956-8400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CARL HILSZ'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Her Attorneys. Granted. Within 15 days, Defendant shall serve further responses and produce responsive documents in a manner that complies with the provisions of the protective order, as follows:
1. Defendant shall produce monthly parking lot diagrams from January 1, 2007 to January 1, 2014 (84 months less the nine months already produce). If no additional diagrams exist, Defendant shall state that a diligent search and reasonable inquiry has been made, identifying what efforts have been made to locate them.
2. Defendant shall produce all parking leases for all 103 spaces from 2007 to the present, including parking lease provisions in commercial leases. Defendant may redact from the amount paid for the commercial lease and other information subject to the right of privacy (i.e. Social Security nos.) However, the amount and details regarding the parking leases are to be produced without redaction.
3. Defendant shall produce all checks or payments showing taxes paid to the City of San Francisco from 2007 to the present. The search shall include all files, which are in the possession custody or control of the defendant, including bank records which may be in the current custody of the defendant's bank(s) and which can be obtained by the defendant by requesting copies.
4. Defendant shall produce accounting records, including the book, legers, spreadsheets and other records which a business customarily keeps in their business operations showing the revenue and expenses of the business from 2007 to the present which specifically relate to the 103 parking spaces. Other accounting records that do not relate to the parking spaces need not be produced.
6. Defendant shall produce all tax returns from 2007 to the present. Of the approximately 28 tax returns, only 19 were produced, and Defendant shall produce the remaining tax returns. If there are no additional tax returns, Defendant shall state that a diligent search and reasonable inquiry have been made, identifying what efforts have been made to locate them. However, the search for the missing tax returns shall include all files, which are in the possession custody or control of the defendant, including records which may be in the current custody of the defendant's accountants, bookkeepers or tax preparers which can be obtained by the defendant by requesting copies.
7. Defendant may produce a privilege log that explains each redaction or withheld document in accordance with CCP 2031.240.
Monetary sanctions in the amount of $ 1,800.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff Carl Hilsz and against Defendants to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/EHG

Off Calendar Jun-06-2013 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jul-30-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Thomas J. Lalanne of Law Offices of Thomas J. Lalanne(415-434-1122). No other appearance.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: DEFENDANT VERA CORT's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The legislature chose to limit its exception for tax matters to those arising under the Revenue and Taxation Code. (Government Code section 12651(f).) The Court cannot assume the legislature made a mistake and broaden its plain and unambiguous exception to include the San Francisco Business and Tax Regulations Code. Additionally, in surreply, Defendant contends that tax liability is not an Obligation that arises from statute or regulation. (Government Code section 12650(b)(5).) Even if the procedural defects of surreplies are ignored, this argument fails on its merits, at least at the demurrer stage. Order signed in open court. Judge Peter J Busch, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D501)
